articles.title: Kanye West Wants to Talk to Jewish People in the UK
articles.summary: Kanye West, a famous rapper, has offered to meet with Jewish community members in the UK after people got upset about him performing at the Wireless Festival. He said he wants to listen and show change through his actions. The Jewish group in the UK said they might meet him if he cancels his festival shows. Kanye has said mean things about Jewish people in the past, but he apologized earlier this year. He is trying to come back to performing, and he recently did concerts in Los Angeles. The UK government is checking if he can enter the country for the festival.

articles.title: A Big Company Gets a Huge Offer to Buy It
articles.summary: Universal Music Group is a very big music company that works with famous singers like Taylor Swift. A company called Pershing Square wants to buy Universal for a lot of money, about $64 billion. Pershing Square is an investment company that already owns part of Universal and other big companies like Google. The boss of Pershing Square, Bill Ackman, says Universal is a great company with many artists and does good business. He thinks buying Universal will help fix some problems, like its stock price not going up as much as it should. If the deal happens, Universal's owners will get cash and shares in a new company listed in New York. The deal might finish by the end of the year, and Universal's stock went up when people heard the news.

articles.title: A Super Strong Computer Chip That Can Handle Extreme Heat
articles.summary: Scientists have made a new computer chip that can work in very hot places, like inside volcanoes or in space. Most electronics stop working when it gets too hot, but this chip keeps going at 700 degrees Celsius, which is hotter than lava. It is called a memristor and is built with special materials like tungsten and graphene. These materials help the chip store information and do calculations without breaking down. The chip was discovered by accident when researchers were trying something else. This invention could help make better robots, cars, and computers that work in tough conditions, changing how we use technology forever.

articles.title: Lil Nas X Gets Help Instead of Jail Time
articles.summary: Lil Nas X, a famous rapper, got into trouble last year when he was arrested for fighting with police officers while walking almost naked on a street in Los Angeles. Instead of going to jail, a judge in California decided to let him join a special program that helps people with mental health issues. If he finishes the program and follows the law for two years, the charges against him will be dropped. The judge said that Lil Nas X, whose real name is Montero Lamar Hill, was acting differently than usual because he has bipolar disorder, a condition that affects his mood and behavior. After the arrest, he spent time in a treatment center to get better. His lawyer and family shared that he has been struggling with his mental health, partly due to his mother's addiction problems. Lil Nas X is thankful for this chance and hopes to improve his health.

articles.title: Katie Boulter Loses Tennis Match in Austria
articles.summary: Katie Boulter, a tennis player from Britain, lost her first match at a tournament in Austria. She played against Elena-Gabriela Ruse from Romania. The match was very close, with both sets decided by tie-breaks. Boulter tried hard, breaking Ruse's serve a few times, but Ruse won 7-6, 7-6. Boulter is ranked 62nd in the world and has won a title earlier this year. This loss means she is out of the tournament early. The article talks about her recent performances in other tournaments too.

articles.title: Savannah Guthrie Returns to TV While Searching for Her Mom
articles.summary: Savannah Guthrie is a TV host who came back to her show called Today. She had been away because her mom, Nancy Guthrie, went missing. Nancy is 84 years old and was last seen at her home in Arizona. Police think someone took her, but they don't know why. Savannah went back to work even though she is sad and worried. Her friends at work welcomed her with signs and kind words. She wore a yellow dress to remember her mom, because people left yellow ribbons at her mom's house. Savannah also talked about her faith on Easter, saying she still believes. She loves her job and calls her coworkers family. The search for her mom is still going on.
